Reproduced: SSE frozen half-open (relay pairs paused, no FIN/error event) at t+0; spec node added server-side at t+1s; board showed ZERO /api/board fetches and never rendered the new node for 75s (pushLive ref stayed true, 15s fallback poll stood down). Control hard-reload immediately showed it (150->151 nodes) — server was fresh, client froze.

Fixed: pushLive liveness detector deleted; 15s fallback poll always runs and rides ETag. Same half-open freeze scenario: quiet polls answered 304 (bodyless) at 15s cadence; after freezing the stream + adding a spec node server-side, the poll's 200 painted it in 9.1s (stream dead throughout, no reload). Status trace: 200,200,304,304,200.

Re-measured at e7468ead (data layer now on conditional loadBoard). Rail + graph tiles + HUD mounted; rail button -> session board (hash #/sessions/new, board lists live sessions), back -> #/graph with tiles; node tile click drills down (dashboard-ui visible); console errors: NONE.

Re-measured at e7468ead. Dead /api proxy (:8933 unbound): after apiFetch backoff the shell rendered the fail-loud panel ('backend unreachable — the board failed to load.' + retry), no eternal spinner. Relay to live backend brought up on :8933, retry click -> board loaded in ~1s.

Re-measured at e7468ead (post-commit HEAD). Self-heal: relay pairs frozen half-open (no FIN/error), spec node added server-side -> board painted it in 9.1s via the always-on poll, stream dead throughout. This run's quiet-window polls answered 200 legitimately (the board WAS changing each tick — this session's own measurement activity updates its record); the 304 half proven in-browser back-to-back: 200 + ETag then If-None-Match -> 304 with body length 0 through the same vite+relay chain (and at 15s cadence in the previous reading).
re-measure after tooltip-system change to styles.css/Dashboard.jsx: rail+HUD render, graph↔sessions switch works (hash #/sessions/new → #/graph), zero console errors

Reproduced through the served dist (spex serve ui :18902): loaded #/graph, rebuilt dist (IssuesPage chunk C-2rEGmJ→D0sdi8QJ), clicked Issues without reloading. Gateway SPA-fallbacked the missing /assets/IssuesPage-C-2rEGmJ.js to index.html → strict module-MIME error + 'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module' → the WHOLE page went blank (uncaught error, no recovery, no auto-reload). Exactly the error reported from the bj01 gateway.

Same rig as the failing reading (served dist via spex serve ui :18902, rebuild rotates IssuesPage chunk hash, click Issues without reloading). Post-fix: gateway answers the stale /assets/IssuesPage-*.js with 404 (curl-probed), vite:preloadError fires, the shell reloads once, and the Issues page renders fully on #/issues. Console carries only the expected one-time 404; no MIME error, no blank app. SPA route fallback (/issues/3 → index.html 200) and gzip asset serving re-probed intact.

same half-open freeze run, watching the CLIENT'S STREAM at the relay: 28.5s after the freeze the heartbeat watchdog tore the dead EventSource down and the relay counted a REPLACEMENT /api/graph/stream conn (#2); after the re-anchor, a second probe node rendered in 0.43s — sub-second push freshness resumed, no permanent poll-only degradation.

repro (issue #70 loop 2): vite client vs fixture backend :18790 (3 online sessions). Push handed a stale full omitting online session 0b230c67; correctives dropped, pings passing. 90s hold: pane count stuck at 2/3, stripped session never returns (recoveredAtS=null). Poll log: 2x200 at boot (ETag latched from a fetch superseded by the pushed stale board), then 5x304 — the fallback poll certifies a board nobody displays; only a hard refresh would recover.

B of the #70 pair (fix at e37ec4e0): same injection (stale full omitting online 0b230c67, correctives dropped, pings passing). Stale display 3/4 panes for 12s; the t=14s fallback poll went UNCONDITIONAL (SSE apply cleared the ETag key) → 200 painted the full board → 4/4 panes, stripped session back on the rail; polls then re-earned 304s (tally 3x200, 2x304). Recovery 14s ≤ one poll period, well inside the 75s wall.

A-side (pre-fix) timer census, real browser (headless chromium via playwright-core) on the worktree vite dev server against the live :8787 backend. The stream watchdog is a POLLING LOOP: /src/data.js registers a 5000ms setInterval (census shows the live one fired 8x over the 35s idle window; 7 fires inside the measured epoch), so liveness detection wakes the tab every 5s even while the link is perfectly healthy — the expected dead-man form (zero idle wakeups, no interval) is violated. resilientSocket.js meanwhile carries its own duplicate SERVER_PING_MS/DEAD_MS pair. streamOpensDuringIdle=0 (stream healthy, no false teardown).

B-side (post-fix) timer census, same rig as the A reading (headless chromium, worktree vite dev, live backend). The 5000ms data.js setInterval is GONE (dataLayerIntervals=[]) and the 35s idle window shows ZERO data-layer timer fires (idleFiresByDataLayer=0) — the SSE watchdog is now the shared heartbeat.js dead-man switch, re-armed by every stream event; the only interval left is App.jsx's 15s fallback poll (a different belt). streamOpensDuringIdle=0: a healthy stream is never falsely torn down. Constants unified: one SERVER_PING_MS/DEAD_MS pair in heartbeat.js, consumed by both data.js and resilientSocket.js.

Post-refactor reconnect-behaviour check (the scenario's half-open mode, realized by SIGSTOP-freezing a throwaway worktree backend's whole process group mid-stream: TCP stays open, no FIN, no error, pings stop). The dead-man switch detected the silent death and opened a replacement /api/graph/stream 22.0s after the freeze — inside one 25s dead window, and without the old poller's up-to-5s check-cadence slack. After SIGCONT the pending reopen was answered (no further stream churn: streamOpensAfterUnfreeze=0) and the board rendered on (screenshot). Break-then-recover behaviour is unchanged by the shared-module extraction; detection just costs zero wakeups now.

Re-measured after the heartbeat unification touched data.js.
